title: Fortressa
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg|italics=1}} {{IndividualShip |image= |name=''Fortressa'' |designer= |manufacturer=[[Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc./Legends|Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc.]]''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Incredible Vehicles]]'' |line= |model=[[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class battleship]] {{C|modified [[Lucrehulk-class LH-3210 cargo freighter/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class LH-3210 cargo freighter]]}}''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' |type=[[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length=3,356.9 [[Meter/Legends|meters]][[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] states that the ''Fortressa'' is 3,000 meters long and three kilometers wide. The [[StarWars.com|official site's]] [http://www.starwars.com/explore/encyclopedia/technology/tradefederationbattleship page] on the Trade Federation battleship gives the vessel's exact dimensions. This article assumes that the ''Fortressa'' complies with these specifications in terms of size. |width=3,009 meters |height=1,028.77 meters |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem=[[Hyperdrive/Legends|Equipped]][[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament= |complement=500 [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] |bays= |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |role=*[[Battleship/Legends|Battleship]] *[[Carrier/Legends|Carrier]] |commission= |destroyed=[[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], [[Horuz system/Legends|Horuz system]] |retired= |battles=[[Attack on the first Death Star]] |affiliation=*[[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] *[[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] *[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |navy=*[[Trade Federation fleet/Legends|Trade Federation Defense Fleet]] *[[Confederate Navy/Legends|Confederate Navy]] *[[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] |fleet= |owners= |captains= |crewmembers= |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|All units, this is Grand Moff Tarkin. We have detected an enemy carrier shifting into realspace from lightspeed in Sector Seven, at two thousand, two hundred kilometers' distance from the station, repeat, enemy carrier in Sector Seven. The vessel is identified as the ''Fortressa'', a ''Lucrehulk''-class carrier. Star Destroyers are moving to engage, but we expect the enemy to launch fighters. They pose a risk to the station. Stop them.|Grand Moff Wilhuff Tarkin briefs TIE starfighter pilots on the threat posed by the ''Fortressa''|}} The '''''Fortressa''''' was a modified [[Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc./Legends|Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc.]] [[Lucrehulk-class LH-3210 cargo freighter/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class LH-3210 cargo freighter]] commissioned by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] megacorp. Converted to a [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|battleship]] as part of the Federation's desire to better [[Trade Federation fleet/Legends|protect its cargo]] [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]], the ''Fortressa'' was later part of the [[Confederate Navy/Legends|naval forces]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]—with whom the Trade Federation had allied themselves—which fought against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The ''Fortressa'' survived the war, and it was decommissioned and sold off when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]—the successor state to the Republic—nationalized the Trade Federation. Falling into the hands of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], the ''Fortressa'' served as [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Carrier/Legends|carrier]] and anchored the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet's]] main battle line. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the ''Fortressa'' [[Attack on the first Death Star|engaged]] the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] in the [[Horuz system/Legends|Horuz system]] in an effort to destroy the immense [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] before it became operational. The [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] used its [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] on the ''Fortressa'', destroying the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] in a single shot. ==Description== {{Quote|Sir, the first wave of enemy fighters has left the carrier. Two hundred and fifty X-wings.|A lieutenant on the Death Star reports the ''Fortressa''{{'s}} activity to Grand Moff Tarkin|}} [[File:Federationbattleship.jpg|thumb|left|A ''Lucrehulk''-class battleship, painted in the livery of the Confederacy of Independent Systems]] The ''Fortressa'' was a modified [[Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc./Legends|Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc.]] [[Lucrehulk-class LH-3210 cargo freighter/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class LH-3210 cargo freighter]], converted by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] megacorp into a [[Battleship/Legends|battleship]] as part of its military build-up. The vessel was officially classed as a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] despite the fact that the [[Anaxes War College System/Legends|Anaxes War College System]] classified Star Destroyers as vessels measuring between 1,000 and 2,000 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] while the ''Fortressa'' measured around three [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] in diameter, 3,356.9 meters long, 3,009 meters wide, and 1,028.77 meters high. ''Lucrehulk''-class [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] resembled a ring with a gap in the front, ending in two truncated prongs that housed massive [[Hangar/Legends|hangars]] and cargo holds. A spherical command section sat in the center of the ring, attached to the rest of the vessel by a rear mounted spar. The [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]] was located in the command sphere, and the [[Engine/Legends|engines]] were positioned at the rear of the ship.{{SWE|technology|tradefederationbattleship|Trade Federation battleship}} By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the ''Fortressa'' served as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Carrier/Legends|carrier]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], and it housed five hundred [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] in its hangars. ==History== {{Quote|Grand Moff Tarkin. We're en route to the station, and it seems there is some interesting activity out there.''"
"''Nothing we can't handle. Though you might want to circle around and avoid it.''"
"''By it, you mean that enemy carrier and all those X-wings pouring out of it?''"
"''Yes. That area is about to become inhospitable.''"
"''You're sending in Star Destroyers?''"
"''I was, but as of this moment, I have a better idea.''"
"''Ah.''"
"''Precisely.|Admiral Natasi Daala confers with Grand Moff Tarkin during the ''Fortressa''{{'s}} approach|}} Originally commissioned as a cargo [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] by the Trade Federation, the ''Fortressa'' was modified to serve as a [[Warship/Legends|warship]] when the megacorp gained approval from the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] to raise a [[Trade Federation fleet/Legends|military arm]] to better defend its [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]]. The ''Fortressa'' was upgraded, joining the ranks of vessels that would become known as [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class battleships]]. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] that raged from [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the ''Fortressa'' served in the [[Confederate Navy/Legends|Confederate Navy]] as the Trade Federation had pledged its allegiance to the Confederacy. The war ended with the enactment of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]]—a [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|contingency order]] that mandated the [[Death/Legends|termination]] of the Republic's [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi]] [[High Jedi General/Legends|commanders]] if they were ever deemed to be a threat to the Republic itself—and the [[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|execution]] of the [[Separatist Council/Legends|Separatist Council]] on [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]]. [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] announced that the Republic was being reorganized into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], with himself as [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]].{{Film|III}} The Empire nationalized the Trade Federation and sold off its [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of Lucrehulks, including those converted battleships like the ''Fortressa'' that had survived the war. While most of the Lucrehulk fleet ended up in commercial hands, the ''Fortressa'' was acquired by the Alliance to Restore the Republic, a resistance movement against the tyrannical and oppressive Empire. The ship served as a starfighter carrier, with five hundred [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]] stationed aboard. Although the early [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] relied heavily on starfighter operations, Alliance [[Chief of State (Alliance)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] insisted on building a [[Navy/Legends|naval]] battle line, which was anchored by the ''Fortressa'' alongside the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] and the modified [[Providence-class carrier/destroyer/Legends|''Providence''-class carrier/destroyer]] ''[[Rebel One]]''. [[File:DeathStar1-SWE.png|thumb|right|The Death Star]] In 0 BBY, the Alliance learned of the location of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], an immense [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] armed with a [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] capable of destroying [[Planet/Legends|planets]]. The [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] was under guard in the [[Horuz system/Legends|Horuz system]], located in the [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]],{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} and the ''Fortressa'' was assigned to [[Attack on the first Death Star|attack]] the station before it became operational and attempt to destroy it using its starfighter complement. From a tactical viewpoint, the ''Fortressa'' presented the best option of success. Rather than use the Alliance Fleet in an attempt to engage the Star Destroyer force guarding the Death Star, which would have been costly in terms of manpower and material for the nascent Rebellion, the large number of starfighters from the ''Fortressa'' had a greater chance of slipping through the defensive screen and inflicting damage upon the battlestation. Even if the attack only delayed the construction of the [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], the Alliance could claim a tactical victory. Decanting from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], the ''Fortressa'' was faced with the Death Star, a Star Destroyer escort, and ten [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] in the middle of a training exercise. When the ''Fortressa'' registered on Imperial [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]], the TIE fighters and Star Destroyers were immediately ordered to intercept, while a further thousand TIEs were launched from the battlestation. The ''Fortressa'' responded by launching 250 X-wings in the first wave, followed by a further wave of equal numbers, totaling five hundred. A squadron of Alliance fighters broke off from the main group to engage a starship carrying [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala]] to the station. The Imperial ship was able to destroy its attackers, but it lost its [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] in the process. An explosion on the command deck caused by the attack injured Admiral Daala, leaving her with a head wound. [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], in [[Commander/Legends|command]] of the Death Star, had initially planned to engage the Alliance ship in a conventional [[Space warfare/Legends|space battle]], but reconsidered the approach and ordered the TIEs and Star Destroyers to break off their intercept. Instead, he communicated with the [[Gunner/Legends|gunnery]] crew manning the superlaser and ordered them to target the ''Fortressa''. The crew complied, and—using only four percent of the weapon's capability—vaporized the ''Fortressa'' in a single shot. The fighters that the carrier had launched continued their mission; the first wave of X-wings outnumbered the initial TIE picket line by two-to-one, and they flew in a non-evasive pattern in an attempt to punch through the TIE defensive screen and attack the Death Star directly. The defending TIEs employed a zone defense, and the X-wings did not attempt to engage the Imperial force, only shooting at TIEs if they were in their path to the battlestation. Around eighty X-wings broke through the first TIE line, but they were soon destroyed by the second wave of Imperial fighters launched from the station. None of the X-wings from the second wave broke through the TIE defensive line. The ''Fortressa'' was the Death Star's first kill, and the first successful test of the battlestation's superlaser. However, the carrier's attack led to Tarkin choosing to relocate the Death Star to avoid detection and a possible full-scale battle with the Alliance Fleet. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Fortressa'' first appeared in [[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']], written by [[Michael Reaves]] and [[Steve Perry]] in [[2007]]. ==Appearances== *[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *''[[Death Star Owner's Technical Manual]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Fortressa }} [[Category:Lucrehulk-class battleships]] [[Category:Starships of the Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[Category:Starships of the Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[Category:Starships of the Trade Federation]]
